title: Zeyheria tuberculosa
intro: Zeyheria tuberculosa, commonly known as the "Brazilian shrub," is a flowering plant species native to Brazil. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Zeyheria tuberculosa include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Zeyheria tuberculosa include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Zeyheria tuberculosa is classified within the plant family Bignoniaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the trumpet vine or catalpa family. Zeyheria tuberculosa falls under the order Lamiales, which includes a large number of flowering plant species. The genus Zeyheria is a group of flowering plants native to southern Africa. Within the plant kingdom, Zeyheria tuberculosa is classified as a vascular plant, or a member of the phylum Tracheophyta.
uses: Zeyheria tuberculosa is known for its potential medicinal properties in traditional medicine. Its bark is used to treat various health issues. Zeyheria tuberculosa is said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help in wound healing and reducing pain. The plant is also used to treat digestive problems and fever. However, further research is required to confirm its efficacy and safety for medicinal use.

side_effects: Zeyheria tuberculosa, commonly known as the wild jasmine or sweetpea vine, has been traditionally used in folk medicine, but its use may come with some side effects.
